devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । तदेवमाकर्ण्य जलेशभाषितं । महामनास्तद् विगणय्य दुर्मदः । हरेर्विदित्वा गतिमङ्ग नारदाद् । रसातलं निर्विविशे त्वरान्वितः ॥ १...
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca tadevamākarṇya jaleśabhāṣitaṃ mahāmanāstad vigaṇayya durmadaḥ harerviditvā gatimaṅga nāradād rasātalaṃ nirviviśe tvarānvitaḥ

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation:
Maitreya said:
Having thus heard the speech of the Lord of Waters (Varuṇa), the haughty and ferocious (Hiraṇyāksa) just ignored it. Having learnt from Nārada the arrival of Hari, Oh Vidura, he hurriedly rushed into Rasātala.[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
saṃviviśe [saṃviviśa]—dived forth for Rasātala with his eyes closed—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: ददर्श तत्राभिजितं धराधरं । प्रोन्नीयमान अवनिं अग्रदंष्ट्रया । मुष्णन्तमक्ष्णा स्वरुचोऽरुणश्रिया । जहास चाहो वनगोचरो मृगः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: dadarśa tatrābhijitaṃ dharādharaṃ pronnīyamāna avaniṃ agradaṃṣṭrayā muṣṇantamakṣṇā svaruco jahāsa cāho vanagocaro mṛgaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: He saw there Hari (conqueror of all) who was holding the earth, and was lifting it up with the tip of his tusks, and surpassing his (Hiraṇyākṣa’s) splendour by his reddish eyes. He laughed out saying, “Oh this is an amphibious beast.”[2]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā states that this apparently provocative speech is the praise ofNārāyaṇa as Hiraṇyākṣa was his attendant in his previous birth. Hence Vana.... mṛgaḥ. He is the Nārāyaṇa who sleeps on waters, and who is sought after by Yogis or who hunts after the wicked for killing—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: आहैनमेह्यज्ञ महीं विमुञ्च नो । रसौकसां विश्वसृजेयमर्पिता । न स्वस्ति यास्यस्यनया ममेक्षतः । सुराधमासादितसूकराकृते ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: āhainamehyajña mahīṃ vimuñca no rasaukasāṃ viśvasṛjeyamarpitā na svasti yāsyasyanayā mamekṣataḥ surādhamāsāditasūkarākṛte

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: He spoke to him, “Come here, Oh fool; leave the earth. This has been given to us, the dwellers of Rasātala by the creator of the universe (otherwise it would not have come down to us). Oh you meanest of gods who have assumed the form of a boar, you cannot get away with the earth safely in my presence.[3]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Eulogistic interpretation: Oh omniscient one (ajña) to whom all gods are inferior, while I am witnessing you setting me at naught, carry away the earth. There is no doubt that you will acquire our prosperous kingdom. But as a favour to us, kindly leave it. You have assumed this boar form as a pastime (līlā)—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā, Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: त्वं नः सपत्‍नैः अभवाय किं भृतो । यो मायया हन्त्यसुरान् परोक्षजित् । त्वां योगमायाबलमल्पपौरुषं । संस्थाप्य मूढ प्रमृजे सुहृच्छुचः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: tvaṃ naḥ sapat‍naiḥ abhavāya kiṃ bhṛto yo māyayā hantyasurān parokṣajit tvāṃ yogamāyābalamalpapauruṣaṃ saṃsthāpya mūḍha pramṛje suhṛcchucaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Are you employed by our enemies for destroying us? You kill Asuras by Māyā and thus conquer them by fraudulent means. Oh dunce, I shall wipe out the sorrows of my friends by killing you, whose strength lies in yoga-māyā, but have little personal bravery.[4]
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Eulogistic interpretation: (i) For the sake of Liberation, you are resorted to by our half-brothers (gods and sages). By your Māyā—power you kill the Asuras and thus kill them from afar (yourself standing aloof). You are the maintainer of the ignorant. You are so powerful by your yoga-māyā that the prowess of others is insignificant before you. I shall establish you in my heart (like a Yogin) and put an end to the miseries of my friends.—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā
(ii) Are you resorted to by our enemies, viz. Sanaka etc. and gods for Mokṣa (as one attains Mokṣa by worshipping the Lord in this world)? No. They cannot, as they are inimical to us, your devotees. I am not your enemy. That you kill Asuras is just to deceive the people. As a matter of fact you give them sāyujya Mukti, for Asura means Yogin. You kill only the sensual ones. You indirectly vanquish the Asuras. I shall establish you in my heart—you who give knowledge to the ignorant. And by concentrating on you, I along with my family will get liberated—Subodhinī

devanagari: त्वयि संस्थिते गदया शीर्णशीर्षणि । अस्मद्‍भुजच्युतया ये च तुभ्यम् । बलिं हरन्ति ऋषयो ये च देवाः । स्वयं सर्वे न भविष्यन्त्यमूलाः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: tvayi saṃsthite gadayā śīrṇaśīrṣaṇi asmad‍bhujacyutayā ye ca tubhyam baliṃ haranti ṛṣayo ye ca devāḥ svayaṃ sarve na bhaviṣyantyamūlāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb]
[nominative dual], [nominative plural], [vocative dual], [vocative plural], [accusative dual], [accusative plural]

translation: When you lie dead with your head shattered by the mace (gadā) hurled by my arms, the gods and sages who worship you with offerings, being uprooted, will be no more.[5]
purport:
Footnote [5]:
When you will be standing at ease with your head unhurt even by the mace struck by our arms, your new devotees who will worship you with offerings and your old devotees, viz. gods and sages, will not be without roots, i.e. will stay firmly established—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: स तुद्यमानोऽरिदुरुक्ततोमरैः । दंष्ट्राग्रगां गां उपलक्ष्य भीताम् । तोदं मृषन् निरगाद् अम्बुमध्याद् । ग्राहाहतः सकरेणुर्यथेभः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tudyamāno daṃṣṭrāgragāṃ gāṃ upalakṣya bhītām todaṃ mṛṣan niragād ambumadhyād grāhāhataḥ sakareṇuryathebhaḥ
translation: Though afflicted by the Tomara—(a javelin) like piercing (sharp) words of the enemy, he put up with that (mental) torment,[6] when he found that the earth on the tip of his tusk was frightened, and he came out of water like a big he-elephant along with the she-elephant when it is attacked by a crocodile.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
(i) Seeing the torment caused to Brahmā and others who put a superficial interpretation on the speech ofHiraṇyākṣa—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā
(ii) He tolerated the apparent hatred in the sharp words piercing like a Tomara (a javelin) as he appreciated Hiranyāksa’s inner devotion.—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: तं निःसरन्तं सलिलाद् अनुद्रुतो । हिरण्यकेशो द्विरदं यथा झषः । करालदंष्ट्रोऽशनिनिस्वनोऽब्रवीद् । गतह्रियां किं त्वसतां विगर्हितम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ niḥsarantaṃ salilād anudruto hiraṇyakeśo dviradaṃ yathā jhaṣaḥ karāladaṃṣṭro gatahriyāṃ kiṃ tvasatāṃ vigarhitam
translation: Just as a crocodile pursues an elephant getting out of water, the Demon with hair of gold, of terrible tusks and of thundering voice, followed him who was rising out of water and roared, “Is there anything reproachful to the shameless wicked?”[7]
purport:
Footnote [7]:
(i) Out of compassion for the frightened earth some flight is not reproachf ul.—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā
(ii) Fie on us (wicked ones) who pursue the Varāha who lifts the earth for the good of the world. What shameful act would shamelessly selñsh persons like me not do?—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: स गां उदस्तात् सलिलस्य गोचरे । विन्यस्य तस्यां अदधात् स्वसत्त्वम् । अभिष्टुतो विश्वसृजा प्रसूनैः । आपूर्यमाणो विबुधैः पश्यतोऽरेः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: sa gāṃ udastāt salilasya gocare vinyasya tasyāṃ adadhāt svasattvam abhiṣṭuto viśvasṛjā prasūnaiḥ āpūryamāṇo vibudhaiḥ paśyato
translation: He (Varāha) placed the earth on the water within the range of perception, and infused in it his power of supporting (mountains etc.). Despite the watching of the enemy (Hiraṇyākṣa), he was praised by Brahmā and was showered over with flowers by gods.[8]
purport:
Footnote [8]:
v.l. viśvasṛjāmprasūnaiḥ—(i) He was praised by gods and sons of Brahmā—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā (ii) He was eulogised by gods who were the flower-like progeny of Brahmā—Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa

devanagari: परानुषक्तं तपनीयोपकल्पं । महागदं काञ्चनचित्रदंशम् । मर्माण्यभीक्ष्णं प्रतुदन्तं दुरुक्तैः । प्रचण्डमन्युः प्रहसन् तं बभाषे ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: parānuṣaktaṃ tapanīyopakalpaṃ mahāgadaṃ kāñcanacitradaṃśam marmāṇyabhīkṣṇaṃ pratudantaṃ duruktaiḥ pracaṇḍamanyuḥ prahasan taṃ babhāṣe

translation: He (the Varāha), feigning extreme wrathfulness, and with a (derisive) laughter, spoke to him (the demon) who was pursuing him with a big mace, and had put on gold ornaments and a wonderful armour of gold, and was constantly wounding him to the quick with harsh words.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । सत्यं वयं भो वनगोचरा मृगा । युष्मद्विधान्मृगये ग्रामसिंहान् । न मृत्युपाशैः प्रतिमुक्तस्य वीरा । विकत्थनं तव गृह्णन्त्य...
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca satyaṃ vayaṃ bho vanagocarā mṛgā yuṣmadvidhānmṛgaye grāmasiṃhān na mṛtyupāśaiḥ pratimuktasya vīrā vikatthanaṃ tava gṛhṇantyabhadra

[nominative dual from √ grah class 9 verb], [nominative plural from √ grah class 9 verb], [vocative dual from √ grah class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ grah class 9 verb], [accusative dual from √ grah class 9 verb], [accusative plural from √ grah class 9 verb]

translation:
The Lord said:
Oh Hiraṇyākṣa, it is true that we are wild beasts in the jungle. I am in search of domestic lions (dogs) like you. Oh evil fellow, warriors do not care for the bragging of yours who are bound down by the cords (noose) of death.

devanagari: एते वयं न्यासहरा रसौकसां । गतह्रियो गदया द्रावितास्ते । तिष्ठामहेऽथापि कथञ्चिदाजौ । स्थेयं क्व यामो बलिनोत्पाद्य वैरम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: ete vayaṃ nyāsaharā rasaukasāṃ gatahriyo gadayā drāvitāste tiṣṭhāmahe stheyaṃ kva yāmo balinotpādya vairam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ dru ], [vocative plural from √ dru ]
[nominative plural from √ dru ], [vocative plural from √ dru ], [accusative plural from √ dru ]
[nominative plural from √ dru ], [vocative plural from √ dru ]
[nominative plural from √ dru ], [vocative plural from √ dru ], [accusative plural from √ dru ]
[nominative single from √ sthā class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sthā class 1 verb]

translation: [9] Here we are, the usurpers of the deposit of the denizens of Rasātala. We are shameless and are made to run away by your mace. (Though unable), we have to take a stand with great difficulty on the battlefield, as stay we must. Where can we go after provoking enmity with the powerful?
purport:
Footnote [9]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā takes these statements as interrogatives implying negative replies: We take away the earth on which you place your foot (i.e. is your support). But is it the property of the dwellers of Rasātala? (No, it is not their personal property). Are we the shameless who are put to flight by you with a mace? (No. We shall make you flee), etc.
Subodhinī interprets differently: We take away the deposit (the earth), as masters do not steal it away like thieves. Those who fly away without putting up a fight and simply boast are shameless. But a person with a sense of shame will not fight with a servant (like you in previous birth), etc.

devanagari: त्वं पद्रथानां किल यूथपाधिपो । घटस्व नोऽस्वस्तय आश्वनूहः । संस्थाप्य चास्मान् प्रमृजाश्रु स्वकानां । यः स्वां प्रतिज्ञां नातिपिपर्त्यसभ...
english_devnagari: tvaṃ padrathānāṃ kila yūthapādhipo ghaṭasva no saṃsthāpya cāsmān pramṛjāśru svakānāṃ yaḥ svāṃ pratijñāṃ nātipipartyasabhyaḥ
translation: You are verily the chief of the leaders of foot-soldiers. Quickly and without hesitation try to defeat us. Wipe out the tears of your relatives by defeating us. He who does not fulfill his vow is not fit for society.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । सोऽधिक्षिप्तो भगवता प्रलब्धश्च रुषा भृशम् । आजहारोल्बणं क्रोधं क्रीड्यमानोऽहिराडिव ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca so ājahārolbaṇaṃ krodhaṃ krīḍyamāno

[nominative single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ krīḍ ]

translation:
Maitreya said:
He who was thus censured and ridiculed by the Lord in anger, grew extremely angry like a big serpent forced to play.

devanagari: सृजन् अमर्षितः श्वासान् मन्युप्रचलितेन्द्रियः । आसाद्य तरसा दैत्यो गदया न्यहनद् हरिम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: sṛjan amarṣitaḥ śvāsān manyupracalitendriyaḥ āsādya tarasā daityo gadayā nyahanad harim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ sṛj class 6 verb]

translation: Being enraged, breathing heavily and with senses agitating in wrath, the Daitya rushing vehemently at Hari, struck him with his mace.

devanagari: भगवान् तु गदावेगं विसृष्टं रिपुणोरसि । अवञ्चयत् तिरश्चीनो योगारूढ इवान्तकम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavān tu gadāvegaṃ visṛṣṭaṃ ripuṇorasi avañcayat tiraścīno yogārūḍha ivāntakam
translation: Just as a Yogin evades the god of death, the Lord, moving aside, parried the blow of the mace aimed at his chest by the enemy.

devanagari: पुनर्गदां स्वां आदाय भ्रामयन्तं अभीक्ष्णशः । अभ्यधावद् हरिः क्रुद्धः संरम्भाद् दष्टदच्छदम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: punargadāṃ svāṃ ādāya bhrāmayantaṃ abhīkṣṇaśaḥ abhyadhāvad hariḥ kruddhaḥ saṃrambhād daṣṭadacchadam
translation: Being enraged, Hari rushed at him who had taken his mace again and was brandishing it, constantly biting his lower lip in anger.

devanagari: ततश्च गदयारातिं दक्षिणस्यां भ्रुवि प्रभुः । आजघ्ने स तु तां सौम्य गदया कोविदोऽहनत् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: tataśca gadayārātiṃ dakṣiṇasyāṃ bhruvi prabhuḥ ājaghne sa tu tāṃ saumya gadayā kovido

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Oh gentle Vidura, the Lord then struck the enemy on his right brow with his mace. But he, a pastmaster in mace fight, returned the blow.

devanagari: एवं गदाभ्यां गुर्वीभ्यां हर्यक्षो हरिरेव च । जिगीषया सुसंरब्धौ अन्योन्यं अभिजघ्नतुः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ gadābhyāṃ gurvībhyāṃ haryakṣo harireva ca jigīṣayā susaṃrabdhau anyonyaṃ abhijaghnatuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ han class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ han class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: In this way, the extremely enraged Hari and Hiraṇyākṣa began to strike each other with their heavy maces for defeating the other.

devanagari: तयोः स्पृधोस्तिग्मगदाहताङ्गयोः । क्षतास्रवघ्राणविवृद्धमन्य्वोः । विचित्रमार्गांश्चरतोर्जिगीषया । व्यभादिलायामिव शुष्मिणोर्मृधः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: tayoḥ spṛdhostigmagadāhatāṅgayoḥ kṣatāsravaghrāṇavivṛddhamanyvoḥ vicitramārgāṃścaratorjigīṣayā vyabhādilāyāmiva śuṣmiṇormṛdhaḥ
translation: When the combatants competed with each other, exchanging heavy blows with their massive maces, and their rage went ablazing at the smell of the blood flowing from their bodies, and began to move in wonderful ways with an ambition for victory, their fight appeared like that of two powerful bulls fighting for a cow, on the earth.

devanagari: दैत्यस्य यज्ञावयवस्य माया । गृहीतवाराहतनोर्महात्मनः । कौरव्य मह्यां द्विषतोर्विमर्दनं । दिदृक्षुरागाद् ऋषिभिर्वृतः स्वराट् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: daityasya yajñāvayavasya māyā gṛhītavārāhatanormahātmanaḥ kauravya mahyāṃ dviṣatorvimardanaṃ didṛkṣurāgād ṛṣibhirvṛtaḥ svarāṭ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ mah ]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]

translation: Oh Vidura, Brahmā surrounded by sages arrived there to see the fight of the combatants aspiring for the earthcombat of the Daitya (Hiraṇyākṣa) and the great (Supreme) Soul who by his Māyā assumed the boar form, the limbs of which are sacrifices.

devanagari: आसन्नशौण्डीरमपेतसाध्वसं । कृतप्रतीकारमहार्यविक्रमम् । विलक्ष्य दैत्यं भगवान् सहस्रणीः । जगाद नारायणमादिसूकरम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: āsannaśauṇḍīramapetasādhvasaṃ kṛtapratīkāramahāryavikramam vilakṣya daityaṃ bhagavān sahasraṇīḥ jagāda nārāyaṇamādisūkaram

[vocative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[imperative active second plural], [injunctive middle third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Seeing that the Daitya who had possessed valour and pride and had lost all fear, offered resistance, and was of irresistible prowess, Lord Brahmā, the leader of thousands of sages, spoke to the Primitive Boar, Nārāyaṇa.

devanagari: ब्रह्मोवाच । एष ते देव देवानां अङ्घ्रिमूलमुपेयुषाम् । विप्राणां सौरभेयीणां भूतानां अपि अनागसाम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmovāca eṣa te deva devānāṃ aṅghrimūlamupeyuṣām viprāṇāṃ saurabheyīṇāṃ bhūtānāṃ api anāgasām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Brahmā said:
Oh God, this is (the demon) who does wrong, inspires fear and does evil to gods, Brāhmaṇas, Kāmadhenus (wish-yielding cows), and innocent beings who resort to your feet. This Asura has obtained boons from me. He is in search of a competent fighter but has found none. He roams over the world troubling the people.

devanagari: आगस्कृद् भयकृद् दुष्कृद् अस्मद् राद्धवरोऽसुरः । अन्वेषन् अप्रतिरथो लोकान् अटति कण्टकः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: āgaskṛd bhayakṛd duṣkṛd asmad rāddhavaro anveṣan apratiratho lokān aṭati kaṇṭakaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ rādh class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ rādh class 5 verb]
[vocative single from √ rādh class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ rādh class 5 verb]
[nominative single from √ iṣ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ iṣ class 1 verb]

translation:
Brahmā said:
Oh God, this is (the demon) who does wrong, inspires fear and does evil to gods, Brāhmaṇas, Kāmadhenus (wish-yielding cows), and innocent beings who resort to your feet. This Asura has obtained boons from me. He is in search of a competent fighter but has found none. He roams over the world troubling the people.

devanagari: मैनं मायाविनं दृप्तं निरङ्कुशमसत्तमम् । आक्रीड बालवद्देव यथाऽशीविषमुत्थितम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: mainaṃ māyāvinaṃ dṛptaṃ niraṅkuśamasattamam ākrīḍa bālavaddeva yathā
translation: Oh God, do not play with him as a child does with an enraged serpent—him who is master of Māyā, haughty, uncontrolled and the wicked-most.

devanagari: न यावदेष वर्धेत स्वां वेलां प्राप्य दारुणः । स्वां देव मायां आस्थाय तावत् जह्यघमच्युत ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: na yāvadeṣa vardheta svāṃ velāṃ prāpya dāruṇaḥ svāṃ deva māyāṃ āsthāya tāvat jahyaghamacyuta

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Oh Acyuta, so long as this terrible (Hiraṇyākṣa) does not grow terrible and unconquerable by resorting to his (Asurī) Māyā at his favourable time, kill him.

devanagari: एषा घोरतमा सन्ध्या लोकच्छम्बट्करी प्रभो । उपसर्पति सर्वात्मन् सुराणां जयमावह ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣā ghoratamā sandhyā lokacchambaṭkarī prabho upasarpati sarvātman surāṇāṃ jayamāvaha
translation: Oh Lord, this most terrible even-tide (evening-time) which is destructive of the world, is approaching. Oh the Soul of all, bring victory to gods before that time.

devanagari: अधुनैषोऽभिजिन्नाम योगो मौहूर्तिको ह्यगात् । शिवाय नस्त्वं सुहृदां आशु निस्तर दुस्तरम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: adhunaiṣo śivāya nastvaṃ suhṛdāṃ āśu nistara dustaram

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation: Now this auspicious period, called Abhijit, which lasts for two muhūrtas has arrived. At least for the good of your friends, the gods, quickly finish with this unconquerable Daitya.

devanagari: दिष्ट्या त्वां विहितं मृत्युं अयं आसादितः स्वयम् । विक्रम्यैनं मृधे हत्वा लोकान् आधेहि शर्मणि ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: diṣṭyā tvāṃ vihitaṃ mṛtyuṃ ayaṃ āsāditaḥ svayam vikramyainaṃ mṛdhe hatvā lokān ādhehi śarmaṇi
translation:
Fortunately this (Hiraṇyākṣa), of his own accord has come to meet death ordained for him. Heroically kill him in the battle, and place (establish) the people in happiness.
